steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog to start creating a custom agent for Microsoft 365 Copilot.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Click the "Default folder" option in the workspace folder selection dropdown within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension. The click is targeted at the highlighted "Default folder" entry, indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (344, 81) near the top of the modal dialog.
Click the "Add Action" menu item under the DEVELOPMENT section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Start with a MCP server" option in the "Add an Action" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code extension for Microsoft 365 Agents.

Type 'https://learn.microsoft.com/api/mcp' into the input field labeled "Enter your MCP server URL (e.g. https://example-mcp.com)" in the VS Code extension prompt after selecting "Add Action" under the DEVELOPMENT section.
Press the Enter key to confirm the MCP Server URL input in the Visual Studio Code extension dialog.

Click the "Start" CodeLens link located above the "servers" object in the `mcp.json` file within Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface. This triggers the MCP Fetch action as indicated by the tooltip.
Click the "ATK: Fetch all from MCP" link at the top of the editor pane in the Visual Studio Code window, within the mcp.json tab, to trigger a data fetch operation from MCP.
Click the "Create a new ai-plugin.json" option in the VS Code quick pick dropdown to initiate creation of a new plugin manifest file.
Press the Enter key to confirm the file name "ai-plugin.json" in the "Name the new action manifest file" input field within Visual Studio Code.
Click the search input field in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to activate it for entering a query.

Click the language selector (flag icon) in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" pop-up at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to change the language or region for Copilot operations.
Use keyboard shortcut: ctrl+shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette while viewing the ai-plugin.json file in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type text 'provision' into the command palette search bar in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
Click the "Provision" command in the command palette dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface, under the "Microsoft 365 Agents" extension, as indicated by the red crosshair.
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface, confirming environment selection for Microsoft 365 Agents development.
@assertion "actions in provision stage executed successfully" exists

Click the "Add Action" button in the "DEVELOPMENT" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel within Visual Studio Code. The target is visually indicated by a blue highlight and a red crosshair.

Click the "Start with an OpenAPI Description Document" option in the "Add an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code extension for Microsoft 365 Agents, as indicated by the highlighted menu and red crosshair.

Type 'https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/' into the "Enter your MCP server URL" input field in the MCP Server URL prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface, ensuring the value is entered where the placeholder text (e.g. https://example-mcp.com) is displayed.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm input in the "Enter your MCP server URL" text field within the Visual Studio Code extension interface.

Click the "SL" code lens button above the line defining "apigithub" in the `mcp.json` file within Visual Studio Code, in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension, to trigger the associated MCP action.

Click the "Allow" button in the GitHub authentication prompt at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface to grant permission for the MCP Server Definition 'apigithubc' to authenticate with GitHub. The red crosshair marks the precise position on the "Allow" button.
Click the "Add Action" option in the DEVELOPMENT section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "ai-plugin.json" file entry in the file selection dropdown titled "Select the action manifest you want to update" within the Visual Studio Code extension interface for Microsoft 365 Agents.

Type 'ai-plugin-1.json' into the "Name the new action manifest file" input field at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, within the "Add Action" workflow of the Microsoft 365 Agents extension sidebar.
Press the Enter key to confirm the file name "ai-plugin-1.json" in the "Name the new action manifest file" input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the search box at the top of the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" dialog in Visual Studio Code to begin entering operation criteria.
Click the "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" dialog within Visual Studio Code to confirm and proceed with the selected operations.
Click the "OAuth (with static registration)" option in the "Select Authentication Type" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code extension interface for Microsoft 365 Agents.
@assertion the file ai-plugin-1.json is opened.
